title: Democrats Struggle to Energize Their Base as Frustrations Mount
content: Even as President Biden achieves some significant victories, Democrats are warning that many of their most loyal supporters see inaction and broken campaign promises.

more_info.byline: By Lisa Lerer, Astead W. Herndon, Nick Corasaniti and Jennifer Medina

en_title: Democrats Struggle to Energize Their Base as Frustrations Mount
en_content: Even as President Biden achieves some significant victories, Democrats are warning that many of their most loyal supporters see inaction and broken campaign promises.

title: The Disconnect Between Biden’s Popular Policies and His Unpopularity
content: Voters often punish a president for pushing an unpopular agenda. But President Biden has been learning that they rarely reward a president for enacting legislation.
en_title: The Disconnect Between Biden’s Popular Policies and His Unpopularity
en_content: Voters often punish a president for pushing an unpopular agenda. But President Biden has been learning that they rarely reward a president for enacting legislation.

title: Seeking Backers for New Fund, Jared Kushner Turns to Middle East
content: Former President Donald J. Trump’s son-in-law is trying to raise capital for his investment firm and is turning to a region that he dealt with extensively while in the White House.

more_info.byline: By Kate Kelly, David D. Kirkpatrick and Alan Rappeport

en_title: Seeking Backers for New Fund, Jared Kushner Turns to Middle East
en_content: Former President Donald J. Trump’s son-in-law is trying to raise capital for his investment firm and is turning to a region that he dealt with extensively while in the White House.

title: United States will bar travelers from 8 countries in southern Africa.
content: Starting Monday, travelers from South Africa, Botswana, Zimbabwe, Namibia, Lesotho, Eswatini, Mozambique and Malawi will be barred unless they are citizens or permanent residents.

more_info.byline: By Zolan Kanno-Youngs and Sheryl Gay Stolberg

en_title: United States will bar travelers from 8 countries in southern Africa.
en_content: Starting Monday, travelers from South Africa, Botswana, Zimbabwe, Namibia, Lesotho, Eswatini, Mozambique and Malawi will be barred unless they are citizens or permanent residents.

title: Interior Dept. Report on Drilling Is Mostly Silent on Climate Change
content: The department recommended higher fees for oil and gas leases, but there was no sign the government planned to take global warming into account when weighing new applications.

more_info.byline: By Coral Davenport and Lisa Friedman

en_title: Interior Dept. Report on Drilling Is Mostly Silent on Climate Change
en_content: The department recommended higher fees for oil and gas leases, but there was no sign the government planned to take global warming into account when weighing new applications.

title: Guantánamo Bay: Beyond the Prison
content: With 6,000 residents and the feel of a college campus, the U.S. Navy base has some of the trappings of small-town America, and some of a police state.

more_info.byline: By Erin Schaff and Carol Rosenberg
des_facet: United States Defense and Military Forces

en_title: Guantánamo Bay: Beyond the Prison
en_content: With 6,000 residents and the feel of a college campus, the U.S. Navy base has some of the trappings of small-town America, and some of a police state.

title: ‘Ed the Trucker’: The Trump Republican Who’s Riding High in New Jersey
content: “If anything, my election showed nobody’s untouchable,” said Edward Durr Jr., who pulled off a stunning victory to win a New Jersey State Senate seat.
en_title: ‘Ed the Trucker’: The Trump Republican Who’s Riding High in New Jersey
en_content: “If anything, my election showed nobody’s untouchable,” said Edward Durr Jr., who pulled off a stunning victory to win a New Jersey State Senate seat.

title: That ‘Team Beto’ Fund-Raising Email? It Might Not Be From Beto.
content: Mimicking official correspondence is an age-old marketing trick. But look-alike emails suggesting links to Beto O’Rourke’s campaign for governor show the tactic has accelerated in the digital era.
en_title: That ‘Team Beto’ Fund-Raising Email? It Might Not Be From Beto.
en_content: Mimicking official correspondence is an age-old marketing trick. But look-alike emails suggesting links to Beto O’Rourke’s campaign for governor show the tactic has accelerated in the digital era.

title: G.O.P. Cements Hold on Legislatures in Battleground States
content: Democrats were once able to count on wave elections to win back key statehouses. Republican gerrymandering is making that all but impossible.
en_title: G.O.P. Cements Hold on Legislatures in Battleground States
en_content: Democrats were once able to count on wave elections to win back key statehouses. Republican gerrymandering is making that all but impossible.

title: Why Retailers Are Fighting a Vaccine Mandate Before the Holidays
content: The Biden administration has called on major companies to help fight the pandemic. Big chains want to get past the holiday staffing crunch first.

more_info.byline: By Lauren Hirsch and Sapna Maheshwari

en_title: Why Retailers Are Fighting a Vaccine Mandate Before the Holidays
en_content: The Biden administration has called on major companies to help fight the pandemic. Big chains want to get past the holiday staffing crunch first.

title: How Far Would Biden Go to Defend Ukraine Against Russia?
content: U.S. officials are vague about when and how they might punish new Russian aggression. President Vladimir V. Putin may doubt their resolve.

more_info.byline: By Michael Crowley and Julian E. Barnes

en_title: How Far Would Biden Go to Defend Ukraine Against Russia?
en_content: U.S. officials are vague about when and how they might punish new Russian aggression. President Vladimir V. Putin may doubt their resolve.

title: Helicopters, Clipboards, Crew Cuts: The President Must Be in Town
content: President Biden and his family are spending Thanksgiving on Nantucket, renewing a family tradition that dates back to 1975.
en_title: Helicopters, Clipboards, Crew Cuts: The President Must Be in Town
en_content: President Biden and his family are spending Thanksgiving on Nantucket, renewing a family tradition that dates back to 1975.

title: The head of Instagram agrees to testify as Congress probes the app’s effects on young people.
content: This will be the first time Adam Mosseri, a trusted lieutenant to Mark Zuckerberg, will appear before lawmakers under oath.

en_title: The head of Instagram agrees to testify as Congress probes the app’s effects on young people.

title: For Afghan Refugees, a Choice Between Community and Opportunity
content: In resettling thousands of displaced Afghans, the Biden administration must weigh their need for support against the needs of the U.S. labor market.

en_title: For Afghan Refugees, a Choice Between Community and Opportunity
en_content: In resettling thousands of displaced Afghans, the Biden administration must weigh their need for support against the needs of the U.S. labor market.

title: Prices climbed in October at fastest pace in 30 years, the Fed’s preferred inflation gauge shows.
content: Prices climbed by 5 percent in the 12 months through October, according to Personal Consumption Expenditures price index data released Wednesday.

more_info.byline: By Madeleine Ngo and Ana Swanson

en_title: Prices climbed in October at fastest pace in 30 years, the Fed’s preferred inflation gauge shows.
en_content: Prices climbed by 5 percent in the 12 months through October, according to Personal Consumption Expenditures price index data released Wednesday.

title: G.O.P. Fights Covid Mandates, Then Blames Biden as Cases Rise
content: Republicans have fought mask requirements and vaccine mandates for months, but as coronavirus infections again rise, they are blaming the president for failing to end the health crisis.
en_title: G.O.P. Fights Covid Mandates, Then Blames Biden as Cases Rise
en_content: Republicans have fought mask requirements and vaccine mandates for months, but as coronavirus infections again rise, they are blaming the president for failing to end the health crisis.

title: Army Whistle-Blower’s Lonely Death Highlights Toll of Mental Illness
content: Ian Fishback revealed abuse of detainees during the Iraq war, but struggled after leaving the service. He died awaiting a bed at the V.A.

title: The Inflation Miscalculation Complicating Biden’s Agenda
content: Administration officials blame the Delta variant for a prolonged stretch of consumer spending on goods, rather than services, pushing up prices and creating a conundrum for the Fed.
en_title: The Inflation Miscalculation Complicating Biden’s Agenda
en_content: Administration officials blame the Delta variant for a prolonged stretch of consumer spending on goods, rather than services, pushing up prices and creating a conundrum for the Fed.

title: Pentagon Forms a Group to Examine Unexplained Aerial Sightings
content: The announcement follows a report that failed to clarify strange phenomena observed by military pilots and others over the past two decades.

title: Biden to Nominate Shalanda Young as Budget Director
content: Ms. Young, the acting head of the Office of Management and Budget, would be the first Black woman to hold the post on a permanent basis.

en_content: Ms. Young, the acting head of the Office of Management and Budget, would be the first Black woman to hold the post on a permanent basis.

title: Order Blocking New York Times Coverage of Project Veritas Stays in Place

title: Steve Bannon and a Cautionary Tale on Congressional Power

title: Rural Health Care Providers Get $7.5 Billion in Relief Funds
content: The money will help offset the increased expenses and revenue losses that rural hospitals and clinics have experienced during the pandemic.

title: Maj. Ian Fishback, Who Exposed Abuse of Detainees, Dies at 42
content: His letter to two senators about beatings by U.S. troops in Iraq led to legislation in 2005 prohibiting extreme mistreatment of military prisoners.

Human Rights and Human Rights Violations
